parsed_review.summary:
CP00-391 is a descriptor-only closeout slice for the RP12 billing program (range RP12.P09.M06.S09-RP12.P09.M10.S10, 84 units, risk C). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is honest and supported. The manifest declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status=implemented_pending_review; the only positive assertion is the descriptor-verified flag (billing_core_p09_closeout_slice_descriptor_verified), which is substantiated.

Verified: (1) Pack binding (CP00-391, risk C, 84 units, upstream CP00-390, next CP00-392, next_subphase RP13.P00.M00.S01, gates H12/C12) is identical across review-baseline, manifest, registry.js, contracts/billing-core-contract.json, the validate script, and tests. (2) All 7 required public exports exist and are implemented (service.js: createBillingCoreCp391P09CloseoutSliceCaseSet/Descriptor; validators.js: validateBillingCoreCp391Coverage, validateBillingCoreCp391P09CloseoutSliceDescriptor, createBillingCoreCp391HermesEvidencePacket/ClaudeReviewPacket/CloseoutHandoff) and re-exported via index.js. (3) Coverage validator enforces first/last unit, unit count, no duplicate IDs, RP12-only, and exact deliverable (impl 55/claude_review 12/security_audit 8/test 4/ui 4/hermes_evidence 1), phase, micro-phase (12/22/20/20/10), micro-title, and per-section row-title distributions; descriptor validator enforces descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false on every row across all 5 sections (spot-checked M06 head and M10 tail in the contract projection). (4) No-write/no-real-data boundary is comprehensive: no DB rows, object storage, audit events, hermes runtime receipts, command/test/UI/API runtime; freeze helpers stamp synthetic_only/no_real_data/writes_product_state on every result. (5) Authority boundary holds everywhere: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true (runtime_opening_pack_id CP00-392). (6) Hermes appears only as a deterministic review-packet/receipt descriptor (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false); not embedded in product runtime. (7) Claude packet is read_only with allowed_tools [Read,Grep,Glob] and carries the CP178+ invalid_review_blockers (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, disabled_tools_run, etc.). (8) The validate script asserts contract<->code parity via deepEqual for current_pack, cp391_requirements, cp391_no_write_attestation, and the full p09_closeout_slice_descriptor projection, preventing drift. (9) command-evidence.json records all required validations plus node --check/--test and git diff --check passing (exit 0); readiness-report shows auth_preflight passed with valid non-zero-byte JSON, so this is not an invalid-auth review. (10) changed-file-scope isolates the 8 active implementation refs from unrelated UI/matter/LDIP dirty files with active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty.

One non-blocking P3: changed-file-scope.json lists the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/cp00-391/ directory (its manifest + command-evidence) under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved while also listing it under untracked_implementation_files - cosmetic labeling inconsistency only. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not final approval authority; human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust/runtime-opening claim.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json dual-lists the pack's own cp00-391 docs directory as both implementation and 'unrelated'
findings.details: The pack's own evidence directory docs/closeout-packs/cp00-391/ (which contains manifest.json and command-evidence.json for CP00-391) is listed under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 138) while simultaneously being listed under untracked_implementation_files (line 132). docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md is likewise in both lists. Labeling the pack's own deliverable directory 'unrelated' is internally inconsistent. This is purely cosmetic metadata in a review artifact and does not affect the pack: active_pack_implementation_refs correctly enumerates the 8 tracked source files, and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so no required pack file was excluded from review scope.
findings.recommendation: Refine the scope generator so the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<id>/ directory is categorized as pack-related (not 'unrelated'), or rename unrelated_dirty_files_preserved to reflect that it is the full preserved-dirty superset. No pack change required; treat as tooling polish.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Does not affect production_ready posture, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, authority boundaries, or review-scope completeness. Pack closeout may proceed.
